title: ( syntax error ) Cannot Read Verilog on new installation with non-default installation path
body:
Hi, I get this error:

> map9v3.v:1: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected $end

when I run: read_verilog map9v3.v

## Steps to reproduce the issue

I downloaded yosys 0.8 (the latest stable build) on the website.
I modified the Makefile PREFIX parameter to something else (a chroot)
I followed the installation steps:

> - sudo make config-gcc
> - sudo make
> - sudo make install

(I had to use sudo or I could not have write permissions to the chroot)
I downloaded the tutorial verilog file "map9v3.v"
inside the directory where map9v3.v was located I ran the following:

> - $ yosys
> - yosys > read_verilog map9v3.v 

The user has map9v3.v read and even write permission. All the yosys dependency were installed both in the main root and the chroot root.
I use ssh to connect to the chroot and the user I use is locked to that chroot. 
The goal is to lock down a user to a certain branch of directories but still let it be able to use yosys.

Note I am using RHEL 7 and that I use a slightly older version of yosys (haven't tried to update it) on the main root, sucessfuly.

## Expected behavior

I would expect yosys to read the verilog file.

## Actual behavior

But instead it says that the file ends on line 1:

```
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend.
Parsing Verilog input from `map9v3.v' to AST representation.
map9v3.v:1: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected $end
```

I can't really tell if it starts reading the file or if gives up before trying.

I tried a few things like changing the permissions on the map9v3.v file or modifying the contents of the file.
My guess is that my chroot is missing a dependency to read the verilog file correctly. The problem is that the error message does not give me enough information on what that dependency might be.
I tried yosys -v 4 but it did not give me a more verbose output.
I tried read_verliog -yydebug map9v3.v but it gives me the same error message

Would any of you have a clue as to where the issue could come from and how it could be fixed. Thank you
code_blocks:
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend.
Parsing Verilog input from `map9v3.v' to AST representation.
map9v3.v:1: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected $end
